Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to generate an event log from the given tables The case ID is order_id We need to produce one row per event

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The events are Order Created from Orders Items Picked from Order Lines Order Dispatched and Order Arrived from Delivery Invoice Issued from Invoice Payment Received from Payments

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Since the picking dates differ within the same order it might make sense to have individual Item Picked events for each line item each with its own timestamp item quantity picker That would be meaningful

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So we will produce one event per order line activity Item Picked Resource is picker_id map to resource_name if available Include item details as attributes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For each delivery we will have a Order Dispatched event with timestamp delivery_dispatch_date and a Order Arrived event with timestamp delivery_arrival_date

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The carrier could be the actor responsible for dispatch and arrival so use carrier_id as resource for both dispatch and arrival events Also include delivery_id and delivery_note as attributes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Invoice Issued from Invoice table each invoice row gives an issue date Activity Invoice Issued timestamp invoice_issue_date resource billing_person_id map Include invoice_id amount as attributes

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Payment Received from Payments table each payment against an invoice Resource could be blank because no direct actor is given We can just leave resource empty and use payment_method as an attribute

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Order creation one event per order Order Created timestamp order_date resource sales_person_id map Attributes customer_id customer_type order_priority

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We need to ensure chronological order

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For process mining a typical flat table can include Case ID Activity Timestamp Resource and then other attributes like item quantity delivery invoice and payment fields with blanks when not applicable

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Resource names are mapped from the Resources table such as Alice Brown Bob Smith David Lin Eva Johnson Frank Miller GlobalTransport FastShip AirExpress Gina Carter Harry Lee and Irene Park

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1001 Order Created then two Item Picked events then Order Dispatched then Order Arrived then Invoice Issued then Payment Received

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1002 Order Created then two Item Picked events then two Order Dispatched events then two Order Arrived events then two Invoice Issued events then two Payment Received events

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003 Order Created then two Item Picked events then Order Dispatched then Order Arrived then Invoice Issued then Payment Received

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1004 Order Created then one Item Picked event and no delivery invoice or payment events because none exist in the tables

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1005 Order Created then two Item Picked events then Order Dispatched then Order Arrived then Invoice Issued then Payment Received

Name: Validation - PE
Text: I will sort all events by timestamp to create a comprehensive event log and double check that all order lines deliveries invoices and payments are included

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Item descriptions and categories can also be joined from the Products table such as Widget A Widget B Gadget X Gadget Y Part P and Special Widget Deluxe

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: I will produce the final markdown table with columns for Case ID Activity Timestamp Resource and relevant attributes including customer item delivery invoice and payment information
